subject: Databases
title: Twitter's Fail Whale Era
description: A short lesson to help you apply Twitter's Fail Whale Era.

hook.text: 2007-2010. Twitter shows a 'Fail Whale' error page constantly. The MySQL database can't handle the traffic.

buildup.text: Every tweet was a write. Every timeline load joined followers and tweets. MySQL wasn't designed for this.

discovery.text: Twitter migrated to a distributed architecture: Manhattan for timelines, FlockDB for the social graph.

twist.text: They didn't abandon MySQL entirely. They still use it — but for appropriate workloads, not everything.

climax.text: Lesson: no single database handles every workload. Polyglot persistence uses the right database for each job.

punchline.text: One database doesn't fit all workloads. Use the right tool for each problem.

quiz.question: How did Twitter solve its database scaling problems?
options: They adopted polyglot persistence, using different specialized databases for different workloads
They bought a bigger MySQL server
They stopped allowing new users to sign up
